pi_items.source: Task description and scripts: export_result.sh line 22 searches for this exact name in VIRTUAL_PRINTER table; verifier.py confirms this is the expected printer name.

Task description (line 5) specifies 'Virtual Printer' type; Floreant POS documentation confirms 'Virtual Printer' is a valid printer type in the system (https://floreant.org/back-office-configuration/). However, the export_result.sh and verifier.py scripts do not actually verify printer type in the database - they only check for printer name and row count.

Code analysis: The exact connection string appears in setup_task.sh lines 20 (in connect statement) and export_result.sh lines 27, 44 (in connect statements). Also defined with -Dderby.system.home flag pointing to /opt/floreantpos/database/derby-server in both scripts.

privileged_info_summary: The task configures a kitchen printer in Floreant POS, a real open-source restaurant Point of Sale system. The expected printer name is 'Kitchen-Expo-1', which is created as a Virtual Printer type in the Floreant system. The system uses Apache Derby database at jdbc:derby:/opt/floreantpos/database/derby-server/posdb. Virtual Printer is a valid and documented printer type in Floreant POS. The task environment is synthetic (created for testing), with Floreant POS downloaded from SourceForge and a pre-populated Derby database. The task verifies success by checking the VIRTUAL_PRINTER database table for the specific printer name and by counting database records.
